name: replay_stop_drop_cycle3
description: 3-cycle replay where the naive compute_breakeven_update return (no previously_persisted_stop_level) would lower stop_level on cycle 3 relative to cycle 2. The ratchet-threaded replay must hold cycle 2's stop level on cycle 3.
provenance: schema-derived + hand-derived arithmetic. All expected values computed from math_engine.compute_breakeven_update invariants: locked_branch => max(base, 0.0); ratchet => max(prev_persisted, resolved). No producer output hardcoded.

cycles.label: lock_fires_at_or_before_this_cycle
cycles.derivation: vol=1.0 => dynamic_activation=1.0; threshold=0.8. current_return=2.0 >= 0.8 => new_hold_ticks=5. 5 >= 5 => LOCK FIRES. locked_branch => stop=max(1.5, 0.0)=1.5.

expected.note: prev=None so ratchet is no-op; stop=1.5

cycles.label: high_watermark_advance_raises_stop
cycles.derivation: locked=True. locked_branch => stop=max(2.0,0.0)=2.0. Ratchet: max(1.5, 2.0)=2.0. No drop.

expected.note: Both agree: stop=2.0. Ratchet has no effect here (new stop > prev stop).

cycles.label: retrace_would_lower_stop_without_ratchet
cycles.derivation: locked=True. locked_branch => stop=max(1.0,0.0)=1.0. WITHOUT ratchet: stop=1.0 < 2.0 (DROP — violation). WITH ratchet: max(2.0, 1.0)=2.0 (hold). This is the E2 invariant: replay must NOT drop below cycle-1 level.

expected.note: The replay simulator must thread prev_stop=2.0 into this call; the math layer then clamps to 2.0. If previously_persisted_stop_level is omitted (current bug), stop drops to 1.0.

assertion_contract.naive_would_violate: stop_level sequence without ratchet [1.5, 2.0, 1.0] has drop at cycle_2
